lesson: Persistently soft product with the compressor always running and low suction pressure is undercharge. The low suction read is the tell, not the soft product alone.
symptoms: Product consistently soft — never quite freezes to serve texture.

tests.error-log: No hard faults. Repeated PRODUCT TEMP HIGH warnings over the past month.
tests.temp-probe: Barrel at −2 °C — should be −6 to −8 °C. Suction pressure low.
tests.continuity-test: All electrical fine. Refrigerant pressures confirm undercharge — suction pressure well below spec.

symptoms: Soft serve more like thick shake since mid-morning.

tests.temp-probe: Barrel at −1.5 °C, should be −6 to −8 °C. Suction pressure well under spec.
tests.continuity-test: Electrics all in spec. Pressures say undercharge — slow leak somewhere.

symptoms: Product fine at open, soft by 11, undrawable by the rush.

tests.error-log: PRODUCT TEMP HIGH warnings clustering later in the day, every day this week.
tests.temp-probe: Barrel can't get below −2 °C under load. Suction pressure low.

authenticityNote: Low refrigerant from a slow leak degrades over weeks; the machine keeps running but can't hit barrel temperature. Low suction pressure is the key read — not the soft product alone, which could be a dozen other things.
